title: sida keralensis
intro: Sida keralensis, a species of flowering plant in the mallow family, Malvaceae, is native to India and is commonly known as the Kerala mallow or Kallum. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Sida keralensis include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, skin disorders, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions that can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
